prompt: Our tests use AnyIO and `pytest-asyncio`, and CI got flaky right after someone set both plugins to auto mode. We also have a module-scoped async fixture for a TCP listener, some tests mutate `contextvars`, one CI job is Windows, and another is PyPy. We want the important tests to run on Trio, plain asyncio, and uvloop where that is actually supported. Show the right pytest setup and tell me what not to do.
assertions: It explicitly mentions the AnyIO vs `pytest-asyncio` auto-mode conflict.
It provides a concrete `anyio_backend` fixture or equivalent configuration.
It includes Trio and plain asyncio in the serious portability matrix.
It treats `asyncio+uvloop` as an additional runtime axis rather than a substitute for Trio coverage.
It explicitly states that a higher-scope async fixture needs a matching higher-scope `anyio_backend` fixture.
It explicitly mentions that AnyIO's test runner keeps async fixtures and tests in the same task, so `contextvars` can leak within that runner.
It gates any uvloop test axis to supported CPython/POSIX runtimes instead of enabling it unconditionally on Windows or PyPy.
